mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a synchronized consumer spending and investment boom that lifts aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined RSAFS-HOUST signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or forward returns turn negative when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, a month-over-month rise in the industrial production index above 0.5 percent indicates resilient manufacturing output that sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above the threshold consistently coincides with S&P 500 drawdowns exceeding 5% over the subsequent 20 days, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.0 percent signals robust economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings per share, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above trend repeatedly fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during standard economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index exceeds the federal funds rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real interest rates turn deeply negative, triggering asset price inflation and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: Real rates below -1.5% consistently coincide with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market strength that reduces recession probabilities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing cycle peak regime, monthly housing starts exceeding 600 thousand units combined with rising real savings indicates excessive credit-fueled consumption that precedes a demand shock, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 600k with rising savings consistently produce positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 days, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after adjusting for interest rate changes.
